### Manusmṛti on Śrāddha

Manusmṛti provides detailed rules for śrāddhas, primarily in Chapter 3 (continued into the provided Chapter 4 excerpt), emphasizing monthly rites, food offerings for ancestral satisfaction, and preferred timings.[^1]

**Foods Used:**
- Basic offerings: Water, milk, roots, fruits (daily); sesame (*tila*), rice (*vrihi*), barley (*yava*), black gram (*masha*), or fruit daily for one month of satisfaction.[^2]
- Meats for extended satisfaction: Fish (2 months), deer/rusa (*mrgayu* 3 months), mutton/goat (*ava* 4 months), birds (*pakshi* 5 months), goat (*chagala* 6 months), spotted deer (*enaga* 7 months), black antelope (*krishna mriga* 8 months), ruru deer (9 months), wild boar/buffalo (10 months), hare/tortoise (11 months), cow milk/rice pudding (*kshirapayas* 1 year), rhinoceros (12 years).[^3]
- Eternal satisfaction (*akshaya*): *Kalasaka* (potherb), *mahasalka* (fish), rhinoceros/red goat meat, honey, hermit foods (milk, roots, fruits); also rice pudding with honey/ghee on trayodasi (13th day), especially rainy season or Magha nakshatra.[^4]
- General: Soups/vegetables (*amvis*/*saka*), milk, curd, ghee, honey, solid/soft foods, roots/fruits, meats, drinks; avoid salty/spiced; hot food preferred.[^5]
- Piṇḍas: Three rice-balls from oblation remnants, offered south-facing.[^6]

**Days Designated:**
- Monthly: *Pindanvaharyaka* at new moon (*amavasya*); *anvaharya* praised with meat.[^7]
- Preferred: Dark half (*krishna paksha*) from 10th excluding 14th (*chaturdasi*); even tithis/nakshatras for desires, odd for progeny; afternoon (*aparahna*) superior to forenoon.[^8]
- Seasonal: Three times yearly (winter [*hemanta*], summer [*grishma*], monsoon [*varsa*]); *ashtaka*, *anvashtaka*, new/full moon sacrifices.[^9]
- Avoid: Night (Rakshasa time), twilights, sunrise.[^10]

### Viṣṇu Smṛti on Śrāddha

Viṣṇu Smṛti details procedures in Chapters 21, 73, and 74, focusing on invitations, oblations, piṇḍas, and timings, with less emphasis on food durations but specifics on ritual variants and female/male distinctions.[^11]

**Foods Used:**
- General: Food soaked in ghee (*sarpiḥ-siktam*), offered in silver vessels; side-dishes (*upaskarana*) like flowers, incense, ointments, edibles/drinks (*poshyam*), honey-ghee-sesame water pitcher (*madhu-ghrita-tilodaka*); fill *karshus* (trenches) with food/curd/ghee/honey/meat (men), food/milk/curd/meat/milk (women).[^12]
- Variants: Vegetables/meat/cakes on *ashtaka* (men/women separately).[^13]
- Ekoddishta/monthly: Single piṇḍa near remnants; *karshus* filled with food/curd/ghee/honey/meat.[^14]
- Piṇḍas: Three (father/grandfather/great-grandfather) on south-tipped kusha; formulas invoke earth/atmosphere/heaven as ladles.[^15]

**Days Designated:**
- General: Forenoon (*prahara*) bright fortnight (*sukla paksha*) or afternoon dark fortnight (*krishna paksha apara*).[^16]
- Ritual variants: Uncooked/optional śrāddhas (first 5 mantras); animal (*pasu*, middle 5); new moon (last 5); post-Āgrāyaṇī/*ashtaka* (first/middle/last in sequence); *anvashtaka*.[^17]
- Monthly ekoddishta: Death tithi every month; *sapindikarana* end-of-year or trayodasi after 12 months.[^18]
- *Ashtaka*/*anvashtaka*: Dark fortnight, with god-rites first.[^19]

### Where Viṣṇu Specifies What Manu Leaves General
Manu provides satisfaction durations by food type but leaves ritual timings and mantra allocations general (e.g., dark fortnight preferred but no precise mantra groups or paksha subdivisions). Viṣṇu specifies **mantra groups for śrāddha subtypes** (uncooked/optional: first 5; animal: middle 5; new moon: last 5; post-festival sequencing), timings (forenoon sukla or afternoon krishna), and *karshu*-fillings distinguishing male (food/water) vs. female (food/milk) lines—details absent or generalized in Manu.[^20]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Chapter 3, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/03/translation/).

[^2]: Manusmṛti 3.267, [p-993e15](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-993e15).

[^3]: Manusmṛti 3.268–271, [p- something, multi-para; use get_passage if needed, but from body].

[^4]: Manusmṛti 3.272–275, [p- (multi)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-973748) etc.

[^5]: Manusmṛti 3.226–228, 257, [p-c5209a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-c5209a).

[^6]: Manusmṛti 3.215, [p- (body)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-2c6784).

[^7]: Manusmṛti 3.122–123, [p-a6dada](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-a6dada).

[^8]: Manusmṛti 3.276–278, [p- (body)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-8b8ea4).

[^9]: Manusmṛti 3.281, [p- (body)](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347
